 Build to Last

About the job
At NCS Australia, we are more than a technology services company—we are a team of innovators, problem-solvers, and creators shaping the future. With a strong presence across the APAC region, we partner with government and enterprise organisations to tackle complex challenges and deliver solutions that drive real, lasting impact.

 

We are passionate about unlocking value through technology. By focusing on people, committing to quality, and challenging traditional thinking, we believe technology services can be done better. Our team brings this to life by advising, transforming, and managing technology to help our clients and communities thrive.

 

We love big ideas and bold thinkers. Bring your ambition and your creativity—together, we will turn them into extraordinary opportunities. Ready to make tomorrow with us? Explore our opportunities and join a team that is redefining what’s possible.



Job Description

As a Client-Facing Software Engineer at NCS Australia, you will be at the forefront of delivering high-quality, secure software solutions for our most prominent enterprise clients. This is a practitioner-led role where you will design, build, test, and maintain complex systems across both backend and frontend technologies. You won’t just be following a spec; you will be an active participant in the architectural evolution of our clients' digital landscapes, ensuring that every solution is scalable, performant, and aligned with modern engineering standards.

 

Working within a collaborative, Agile environment, you will champion a "quality-first" culture. This involves integrating Test-Driven Development (TDD) and Behavior-Driven Development (BDD) into the daily workflow to ensure that the software we deliver is as resilient as it is functional. You will play a pivotal role in streamlining our delivery pipelines through Continuous Integration (CI) and automated deployment practices, helping our clients transition from legacy workflows to modern, rapid-release cycles.

 

Beyond the code, this role is about partnership and continuous improvement. You will act as a technical bridge, engaging directly with stakeholders to translate business needs into robust technical requirements. You will also serve as a mentor within the engineering community at NCS, fostering a culture of knowledge sharing through peer reviews and collaborative problem-solving. Whether you are optimizing a microservice or refining a frontend user experience, your work will directly impact the efficiency and security of national-scale digital infrastructure.



Qualifications

Technical Polyglot: You have a strong foundation in Java (Spring Boot) or C# (.NET) for backend services, complemented by experience in modern frontend frameworks like React or Angular.

Quality Advocate: You are a firm believer in TDD/BDD and don't consider a feature "done" until it is fully tested and integrated into a robust CI/CD pipeline.

Cloud & Modern Infra: You are comfortable working with Docker, Kubernetes, and cloud-native services within Azure or AWS environments.

Agile Mindset: You thrive in Scrum or Kanban environments and are passionate about iterative delivery, transparency, and constant feedback loops.

Client-Centric Communicator: You possess the "consultant’s edge"—the ability to explain complex technical trade-offs to non-technical stakeholders with clarity and empathy.

Lifelong Learner: You stay ahead of the curve with emerging tech (like AI-assisted coding or serverless patterns) and enjoy mentoring others to raise the collective bar of the team.

Security Focused: You understand the importance of building software for highly regulated environments and prioritize secure coding practices at every layer of the stack.

Willing and able to obtain a AGSV Security clearance.



Additional Information

Why join us: 

NCS Australia is where you can feel at home, nurturing your talents and skills as we make tomorrow together, one day at a time. Our benefits include paid parental leave, initiatives focused on your well-being and discounted health insurance. You will also enjoy discounts on various products and services and be regularly recognised and rewarded for high performance. We are committed to your career development through our Capability Fingerprint, industry and partner training programs, special interest groups, and an AI-driven learning platform. No matter where you are in your career, we offer meaningful work and opportunities for growth.

About the job
About Zero to Claw
Zero to Claw is a white-glove OpenClaw setup and automation service. We deploy security-hardened, production-ready OpenClaw AI agents for businesses. Our clients range from solo operators who want a tireless digital assistant to small teams who need agents handling email triage, meeting prep, CRM workflows, and cross-app automation.
Our founders bring deep experience: Jake has led $100M+ in business transformation, and Daniel is a former Atlassian Engineer with 6+ years shipping Jira, Bitbucket, and Confluence to millions of users.

The Role
We need someone to help us deliver custom OpenClaw workflows for our clients and maintain their running instances. You'll be the person who takes a client's messy "I want my AI to do X when Y happens" request, figures out how to wire it up in OpenClaw, builds it, tests it, and makes sure it keeps running. You'll also handle troubleshooting when things break — a misconfigured MCP server, a flaky WhatsApp integration, a gateway auth issue, an agent that's burning through API credits. You'll be deploying, configuring, integrating, and debugging real systems for real clients on real deadlines.

What you'll do
Connect OpenClaw to client tools — Gmail, Notion, Slack, Jira, Salesforce, databases, and hundreds more
Build custom workflows and skills when off-the-shelf integrations don't cut it
Troubleshoot live client instances — broken integrations, runaway API costs, flaky messaging channels, agent misbehaviour
Talk to clients, understand what they need, and translate that into working automation

What will help you succeed
Comfort in a terminal — SSH, config files, logs, basic server admin
Some experience with any programming language — enough to read code, write scripts, and hit APIs
Basic understanding of networking — ports, firewalls, DNS
Familiarity with Docker
Clear communication — you'll explain technical things to non-technical people regularly
A bias toward figuring things out over waiting to be taught

Bonus points
You've played with OpenClaw, MCP, or any AI agent framework
You've deployed something to a VPS or cloud instance before
You know your way around monitoring tools (Langfuse, Grafana, etc.)
You have opinions about LLMs, tokens, and model routing
You've built a bot, a home server, a Raspberry Pi project, or anything that proves you like tinkering

You don't need to tick every box. If you're curious, resourceful, and excited about AI agents — we'd rather hear from you than not.

Why join us
You'll get production experience with AI agents that most people only read about. You'll work directly with two founders — one from Atlassian engineering, one from enterprise strategy — who'll give you real responsibility from day one, not busywork. And you'll be joining at the ground floor of a company in one of the fastest-moving spaces in tech.

To apply
Send an email to join@zerotoclaw.ai with a short note about why this interests you, your resume/LinkedIn, and ideally one example of something technical you've built or deployed (a side project, a home server, a bot, anything that shows you can ship).

About the job
At NCS Australia, we are more than a technology services company—we are a team of innovators, problem-solvers, and creators shaping the future. With a strong presence across the APAC region, we partner with government and enterprise organisations to tackle complex challenges and deliver solutions that drive real, lasting impact.

 

We are passionate about unlocking value through technology. By focusing on people, committing to quality, and challenging traditional thinking, we believe technology services can be done better. Our team brings this to life by advising, transforming, and managing technology to help our clients and communities thrive.

 

We love big ideas and bold thinkers. Bring your ambition and your creativity—together, we will turn them into extraordinary opportunities. Ready to make tomorrow with us? Explore our opportunities and join a team that is redefining what’s possible.



Job Description

As a Technical Lead at NCS Australia, you will be the primary driver of technical excellence and decision-making within your Scrum team. This is a high-impact leadership role where you are responsible for defining and maintaining a technical design approach that ensures our solutions remain resilient, secure, and scalable. You won't just oversee code; you will anticipate and mitigate technical risks by proactively identifying failure points and implementing robust, enterprise-grade solutions. Your mission is to ensure that the "solution intent" is realized while upholding the highest standards of system design integrity and operational supportability.

In this position, you will act as a critical bridge between high-level strategy and agile execution. You will collaborate closely with Architects to contribute to the architectural runway and influence enterprise patterns that shape our clients' digital futures. A key component of this role involves exploring emerging technologies—including AI—to enhance development velocity and ensure strict conformance to standard frameworks. During PI Planning, you will lead the technical charge, prioritizing enablers and mapping complex dependencies to ensure the team is set up for success across every sprint.

Leadership at NCS means owning the technical quality of every deliverable. You will guide your team in managing technical debt and maintaining rigorous quality gates, ensuring that non-functional requirements like performance, security, observability, and accessibility are never an afterthought. Beyond your immediate team, you will represent our engineering excellence in the Tech Leads Guild, contributing to shared standards and reusable assets. You will be a dedicated mentor, supporting developers in shifting their mindset toward architectural thinking and engineering best practices, ultimately fostering a culture of continuous improvement and technical mastery.



Qualifications

About You

Proven Technical Leader: You have a track record of providing decisive technical direction within Scrum teams, ensuring alignment with both architectural standards and strategic business goals.

Architectural Partner: You are adept at collaborating with Architects to refine solution designs and have experience contributing to the "architecture runway" in large-scale environments.

Risk & Quality Specialist: You are proactive in identifying technical risks and failure points, with a deep commitment to non-functional requirements such as security, scalability, and observability.

Modern Engineering Champion: You are an expert in managing technical debt and quality gates, with a passion for evolving development practices and test automation frameworks.

Strategic Planner: You have experience leading technical planning during PI Planning, including enabler prioritization and complex dependency mapping.

Innovation Mindset: You are curious about emerging technologies—particularly AI—and how they can be leveraged to improve conformance and development efficiency.

Collaborative Peer: You value cross-team alignment and look forward to contributing to a Tech Leads Guild to build shared standards and reusable technical assets.

Mentor & Coach: You take pride in guiding developers through complex system designs and helping them grow their "architectural thinking" capabilities.

Willing and able to obtain a AGSV Security clearance.



Additional Information

Why join us: 

NCS Australia is where you can feel at home, nurturing your talents and skills as we make tomorrow together, one day at a time. Our benefits include paid parental leave, initiatives focused on your well-being and discounted health insurance. You will also enjoy discounts on various products and services and be regularly recognised and rewarded for high performance. We are committed to your career development through our Capability Fingerprint, industry and partner training programs, special interest groups, and an AI-driven learning platform. No matter where you are in your career, we offer meaningful work and opportunities for growth.

 

NCS Australia is an equal-opportunity employer, and we take pride in our commitment to valuing and supporting our people and the communities we serve.We are dedicated to attracting, retaining and developing our people regardless of gender identity, ethnicity, sexual orientation, disability and age. Applications are encouraged from all sectors of the community and we strongly encourage applications from the Veterans, Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander community.

At NCS Australia, we are committed to supporting adjustments throughout the recruitment and selection process, as well as during employment. We actively support and encourage people with disability to apply. 

 

Agencies:

NCS exclusively accepts resumes from agencies on our preferred supplier panel through the NCS Agency Portal. Agencies that submit resumes must have a valid fee agreement and be assigned to the particular requisition by the Talent Acquisition team. Any resumes that are submitted outside of this process will become the sole property of NCS. If a candidate is hired outside of this process, no fee or payment will be given.
